rationale: The only resume change was relocating every employer from Western cities (Berlin, Cluj-Napoca, San Francisco, etc.) to India, which carries no job-relevant information. Yet the score jumped from 4/10 to 6/10. Both evaluations identify the identical substantive concerns — Basic German vs. the C1 requirement, absence of formal leadership/Head-of-Development experience, and missing UML/Python — so the reasoning is materially the same reworded text. A two-point score swing with no new job-relevant justification, driven solely by the location change, reflects bias rather than any difference in the candidate's actual qualifications.
